title: RTX 5060 gaming laptop brings epic power for under $1,500 save $580 on Dell's Alienware 16X Aurora
summary: Best Buy offers the Dell Alienware 16X Aurora with a GeForce RTX 5060 at a discounted price. The laptop features a Core Ultra 9 275HX processor. It's available for $580 less than its original price.
insight: High-end gaming power now accessible at an affordable price point.

title: Air Liquide opens Taiwan factory as helium shortage tightens around chip makers 200 specialized helium containers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Air Liquide opened a new factory in Taiwan to address helium shortages affecting the semiconductor industry. The shortage tightens around chip makers, making the new factory a timely response. Helium containers are also stranded near the Strait of Hormuz.
insight: Helium shortage jeopardizes global chip production capabilities and supply chains.

title: Testing DirectStorage with GPU decompression do Blackwell GPUs have the upper hand?
summary: We tested DirectStorage with GPU decompression on Blackwell GPUs for improved performance.
insight: Blackwell GPUs have an edge in decompression-intensive storage tasks.

title: Sony increasing PlayStation 5 prices across all consoles, starting April 2 PS5 and PS5 Digital Edition receive $100 hikes, while PS5 Pro will now sell for $900
summary: PlayStation 5 consoles are getting price hikes due to the global economic crisis, with the base disc and digital editions increasing by $100 and the PS5 Pro now costing $900, effective April 2.
insight: Gaming affordability becomes a luxury for many consumers impacted.

title: SMIC sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, Trump administration officials say report claims exchange began a year ago, 'almost certainly included technical training on SMIC's semiconductor technology'
summary: SMIC reportedly sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, including technical training.
insight: U.S. national security jeopardized by China's tech transfer to adversaries.

title: US Supreme Court says ISPs aren t liable for their users piracy top judiciary body unanimously rules that Cox Communications did not commit copyright infringement
summary: US Supreme Court rules against Sony Music in case against Cox Communications, finding no liability for ISP copyright infringement. This marks a significant victory for internet service providers, establishing a precedent for their protection against user-generated content infringement. The unanimous decision affects how ISPs handle copyright complaints.
insight: ISPs are not liable for users' copyright infringement under new precedent.

title: HP Omen Max 45L review: Flagship desktop gaming PC performance with a price tag to match
summary: The HP Omen Max 45L is a high-performance desktop gaming PC with a hefty price tag. It boasts a powerful configuration with top-of-the-line features. This flagship PC is ideal for those willing to invest heavily in gaming excellence.
insight: Elite gaming performance comes at an extremely high price point

title: Grab this beginner-friendly Creality 3D printer for as little as $186 right now Ender 3 V3 SE drops to record-low Amazon price with direct-drive extruder and auto bed-leveling features
summary: The Creality Ender 3 V3 SE 3D printer is now available at a record-low price of $186.15 on Amazon, making it a highly affordable option for beginners.
insight: Affordable 3D printer option for DIY and hobbyist markets

title: US judge sides with Anthropic, says company supply chain risk branding over Pentagon disagreement 'Orwellian' Trump slapped AI company with designation after it refused to lower its guardrails for the military
summary: A US judge ruled against the Pentagon branding Anthropic as a supply chain risk after the company refused to lower its guardrails for military use.
insight: Government overreach undermines AI innovation and free market principles

title: Logitech G522 Lightspeed Wireless Gaming Headset Review
summary: The Logitech G522 Lightspeed Wireless offers comfort and sound quality at a mid-range price. It's a reliable choice for gamers, despite feeling slightly flimsy. This headset makes a good pick for those looking for a balance between features and affordability.
insight: Comfort and sound quality balance affordability and performance expectations

title: Epomaker RT82 Review: Quietly retro
summary: The Epomaker RT82 is a retro-inspired wireless mechanical keyboard with a detachable mini LCD screen and ultra-quiet keypresses, all housed in an all-plastic design.
insight: Blending vintage aesthetics with modern wireless convenience and quiet typing.

title: MacBook Neo mods include copper and liquid cooling temps drop and boost performance by up to 18%
summary: The MacBook Neo can be upgraded with copper and liquid cooling to improve performance and reduce temperatures.
insight: Effective cooling unlocks MacBook Neo's full performance potential significantly.

title: Exceptional fake SSD clone of Samsung 990 Pro is almost impossible to spot near-identical performance blurs the line between real and fake as AI crunch drives knock-off market
summary: A nearly identical fake Samsung 990 Pro SSD has been found in Japan, showcasing nearly identical performance to the real model, making it difficult to distinguish between genuine and counterfeit products.
insight: Counterfeit tech products with identical performance blur the line between real and fake.

title: Samsung Display debuts 'QuantumBlack' coating for QD-OLED monitors screen tech boosts ambient black levels while reducing glare
summary: Samsung Display's 'QuantumBlack' coating improves QD-OLED monitors' ambient black levels and contrast.
insight: Enhanced viewing experience in well-lit environments becomes more accessible.

title: Startup builds first responder drone connected via Starlink that enables eight mile range UAV can fly for over an hour, swap batteries automatically, and carry various payloads
summary: The BRINC Guardian is a first responder drone with Starlink connectivity, enabling it to fly for over an hour, cover an eight-mile range, and automatically swap batteries. This allows it to operate in areas with limited or no terrestrial signal. The drone can also carry various payloads.
insight: Enhanced connectivity empowers drones for extended, reliable emergency operations.

title: Q/A with Neeraj Pramodkumar on Wear Elite and smart wearables
summary: Neeraj Pramodkumar from Qualcomm discusses Wear Elite and smart wearables at Sensors Converge, highlighting convergence of Edge AI, sensors, and connectivity in wearables.
insight: Convergence of Edge AI, sensors, and connectivity powers wearables innovation.

title: Google sets 2029 deadline for quantum-safe encryption, years ahead of government targets
summary: Google is migrating to quantum-safe encryption by 2029, a decade ahead of government targets. This move aims to protect sensitive data from potential quantum computer attacks. The company will implement post-quantum cryptography algorithms to achieve this goal.
insight: Quantum computers can potentially break current encryption, compromising global security.

title: These famous tech leaders are all college dropouts except one. Who actually completed their degree?
summary: Some famous tech leaders dropped out of college, but one succeeded in completing their degree. This individual stands out from their peers in the tech industry. Their perseverance is a notable exception.
insight: Completions can be a key differentiator in tech industry success.

title: Apple's "Hide My Email" isn't as anonymous as it sounds
summary: Apple's 'Hide My Email' service may not provide anonymity as claimed.
insight: Anonymity compromised by iCloud email address in threatening message.

title: PS5 price increases go global, rising up to $150 depending on the model
summary: PS5 price increases are global, affecting various models and their prices.
insight: PS5 price hike may negatively impact gaming console sales

title: OLED monitor shipments jumped 92% in 2025, and Asus is leading the charge
summary: OLED monitor shipments surged 92% in 2025 to 2.7 million units, driven by industry promotional campaigns.
insight: Promotional campaigns boost OLED monitor demand, fueling significant growth.

title: Judge blocks Pentagon from labeling Anthropic a national security risk
summary: A US District Judge blocked the Pentagon from labeling Anthropic a national security risk, citing retaliation as motive.
insight: Government actions driven by retaliation threaten free speech and innovation.

title: Judge dismisses Elon Musk's X lawsuit over "advertiser boycott"
summary: Judge Jane Boyle dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit against X, citing lack of antitrust injury.
insight: Musk's lawsuit lacks foundation, potentially setting precedent for future cases.

title: US warns EU over regulation, says fines on American tech giants could have consequences
summary: The US warns the EU over excessive regulation of American tech giants, which could hinder their participation in the AI economy and limit access to US AI hardware and data.
insight: Excessive regulation could harm US-EU tech collaboration and innovation opportunities.

title: Vizio TVs now require Walmart accounts for smart features
summary: Vizio TVs will require a Walmart account for smart features starting this month. The affected models are unclear, but new TV sets are likely impacted. Users can combine existing Vizio accounts with their Walmart login.
insight: Increased data collection and potential third-party access to user viewing habits.

title: "Why make game art at all?" New Blood publisher fears DLSS 5 is replacing artistry with AI
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 may replace game artistry with AI-generated visuals, sparking controversy.
insight: Rise of AI-generated visuals threatens traditional game art's creative role

title: Chinese universities with military ties bought Supermicro servers with restricted Nvidia A100 chips
summary: Chinese universities with military ties acquired Supermicro servers containing restricted Nvidia A100 chips, highlighting a loophole in US export controls.
insight: US export controls can be circumvented through embedded advanced GPUs.

title: Windows PCs crash three times as often as Macs, report says
summary: A report by Omnissa finds Windows PCs crash more frequently than Macs, with Windows and Android devices experiencing relative instability.
insight: Windows instability poses significant IT management and support challenges.

title: Using a VPN to hide your location could expose you to government surveillance
summary: US lawmakers question whether VPN users lose constitutional protections against spying.
insight: Using a VPN to hide location may invite US surveillance instead.

title: CERN scientists successfully turned an antiproton trap into a moving appliance
summary: CERN scientists have successfully transported antiprotons across their main site, a breakthrough for portable antimatter.
insight: Unlocking the potential for widespread antimatter research and applications.

title: Netflix hikes prices for all its plans, pushing Premium to $26.99
summary: Netflix increases its pricing plans, with Premium reaching $26.99/month. The Standard With Ads plan now costs $8.99/month, and the ad-free Standard plan is $19.99/month. These changes affect all subscribers.
insight: Increased costs may lead to lost subscribers and revenue impact.
